meta.professor_methodology: Emphasis on sign conventions and ray tracing
meta.description: Reflection, refraction, mirrors, and thin lenses using ray approximation

patterns.name: Thin Lens Equation Applications
patterns.recognition_signal: Image formation by lenses or mirrors with object distance given
patterns.logic: Lens equation relates object distance, image distance, and focal length

efficiency_trick.name: Image Type Quick Check
efficiency_trick.trigger: Determining real vs virtual, upright vs inverted

pitfalls.name: Sign convention errors
pitfalls.description: Real is positive for d_i, virtual is negative

worked_example.title: Converging Lens Image
worked_example.problem_latex: Object at 30 cm from converging lens with f = 10 cm. Find image.

decision_tree.question: Lens or mirror?
decision_tree.no: Mirror: same equation, check sign conventions

professor_tips: Always draw a ray diagram even if not required - it helps check your answer
Memorize the sign conventions: real image = positive d_i
Diverging lenses ALWAYS produce virtual, upright, reduced images
Magnification sign tells you if image is upright (positive) or inverted (negative)

confidence_checklist: I can apply the thin lens equation
I understand sign conventions for lenses and mirrors
I can determine if an image is real or virtual
I can trace principal rays for lens diagrams
